step_by_step_thinking:
The McRoberts maneuver involves sudden hyperflexion of the thigh over the abdomen, leading to stretching and potential compression of nerves in this position. Given the anatomy described, the lateral femoral cutaneous nerve, which provides sensation to the outer part of the thigh, could potentially get stretched or compressed in this movement. Therefore, considering the distribution and sensitivity of the lateral femoral cutaneous nerve:
\boxed{B} Based on the description and involvement of specific nerves in the thighs' peripheral distributions, the lateral femoral cutaneous nerve fits best as it covers much of the anterolateral surface of the thigh and could indeed suffer from stretching or compression during the McRoberts maneuver.
